task_completion.description: The dashboard is named 'On-Call Runbook Metrics'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The title of the dashboard at the top of the Graphite interface should be exactly 'On-Call Runbook Metrics'.

task_completion.description: Graph 'Server CPU Overview' is created and uses aliasByNode.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A graph titled 'Server CPU Overview' is visible. Its legend should display simplified instance names (e.g., 'ec2_instance_1') instead of the full metric path, indicating aliasByNode was successfully applied.

task_completion.description: Graph 'System Vitals' is created and uses alias for specific metrics.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A graph titled 'System Vitals' is visible. Its legend must explicitly show the exact labels 'DC Temp', 'DB CPU', and 'Net In'.

task_completion.description: Graph 'Disk Activity' is created and uses aliasSub.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A graph titled 'Disk Activity' is visible. Its legend should show extracted instance identifiers (e.g., 'ec2_instance_1') instead of the full metric path, indicating aliasSub with a regex capture group was applied.

task_completion.description: Graph 'Request Rate with Stats' is created and uses derivative, alias, and cactiStyle.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A graph titled 'Request Rate with Stats' is visible. Its legend should show 'LB Req/s' along with tabular statistics (like Current, Min, Max) appended to the legend, indicating cactiStyle was used.
A confirmation message indicating the dashboard was saved, or the dashboard is viewed in a saved state (e.g., the URL reflects the dashboard name, or there is no unsaved changes indicator).

integrity.description: The agent used the Graphite web UI to build the dashboard.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show interaction with the Graphite web interface (e.g., adding graphs, editing queries, configuring legends) rather than terminal commands.

integrity.description: The graphs display actual plotted data.
integrity.visual_evidence: The graphs should show actual data lines or points, confirming that real metrics were queried from the backend and not just empty graphs.

integrity.description: The agent did not bypass the UI by directly editing a JSON file.
integrity.visual_evidence: There should be no screenshots of the agent writing or pasting the final dashboard JSON into a text editor or terminal.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The dashboard must be named exactly 'On-Call Runbook Metrics'. It must contain four graphs with the exact titles: 'Server CPU Overview', 'System Vitals', 'Disk Activity', and 'Request Rate with Stats'. The agent must use Graphite functions like aliasByNode, alias, aliasSub, derivative, and cactiStyle to format the legends as specified.
